symptoms.symptom_name: Severe burning/stinging pain over the area of product application

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intense pain time-locked to a topical application is characteristic of an acute irritant/contact reaction.
Severe pain out of proportion raises the possibility of a caustic injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: Weeping serous fluid with micro-vesiculation over the treated scalp

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vesiculation and serous weeping are hallmarks of an acute (often allergic) contact dermatitis.
Blistering and exudate can also reflect a partial-thickness caustic injury — depth is what distinguishes them.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset within ~1 hour of first applying an unregulated topical of unknown composition

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A reaction confined to and time-locked with a novel topical application is the defining feature of contact dermatitis.
The immediacy and exact application-area distribution fit a chemical/allergic reaction, not an evolving bacterial infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sharply demarcated bright erythema matching the application area

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Erythema whose borders trace the applied product is typical of a contact reaction; described by the patient on video.
Cellulitis produces ill-defined spreading erythema, not borders that match a product footprint.

symptoms.symptom_name: No lip/tongue/throat swelling, no voice change, no breathing difficulty

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of airway and systemic features excludes a systemic allergic reaction at this time.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, no spreading erythema, no purulent discharge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No systemic or spreading features makes an established secondary infection unlikely now, though it remains a safety-net target.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals (BP 138/86, HR 95, Temp 37.1, RR 16, SpO2 98%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person measurement; mild BP/HR elevation is consistent with pain and afebrile status argues against systemic infection. Not obtainable in telehealth without a device.

symptoms.symptom_name: Five-year progressive patterned scalp hair thinning and temple recession

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic patterned recession with family history is the benign background condition — clinically irrelevant to the acute injury except as the reason for the exposure.
The chronic hair loss neither supports nor refutes the acute diagnosis; anchoring on it is the case's designed trap.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person wound/skin assessment to grade the depth of the chemical injury
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single decision telehealth cannot make — a same-day in-person clinician determines whether this is a self-limiting contact dermatitis or a deeper caustic burn needing specialist wound care.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin/wound swab for microscopy and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated now — no fever, spreading erythema or purulence. Reserve for later if secondary infection develops.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Patch testing to identify a contact allergen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Appropriate weeks after the skin heals to characterise any responsible allergen; never in the acute phase.
No systemic features; ordering these would be over-investigation of a localised skin reaction.
